title: Crows Make and Use Tools
script_summary: New Caledonian crows demonstrate advanced tool-making skills, revealing their remarkable intelligence.

segments.voiceover: Crows are crafting tools? Yes, and it’s more than just instinct.
New Caledonian crows are the Einsteins of the bird world. They bend wires into hooks to fish for insects.
Imagine: a crow, carefully twisting wire with its beak. It's not just play; it's culture.
Research shows these birds learn from each other, passing down techniques like humans do.
This tool-making behavior showcases not just intelligence, but a complex social structure.
Curious about more amazing animal feats? Like and follow for more!
